concepts.title: Arithmetic Mean
concepts.conceptBody:
Arithmetic Mean for Ungrouped Data

The arithmetic mean is the amount secured by dividing the sum of values of the items in a series by their

(i) Direct method: If the series in this case be ${{x}_{1}},\,{{x}_{2}},\,{{x}_{3}},\,......,\,{{x}_{n}}$; then the arithmetic mean $\bar{x}$ is given by

$\bar{x}=\frac{\text{ Sum of theseries }}{\text{ Number of terms }}$

$\bar{x}=\frac{{{x}_{1}}+{{x}_{2}}+{{x}_{3}}+\ldots .+{{x}_{n}}}{n}=\frac{1}{n}\sum\limits_{i=1}^{n}{{{x}_{i}}}$

(ii) Short cut method 

$(\bar{x})=A+\frac{\sum{d}}{n}$,

where, A = assumed mean, d = deviation from assumed mean = x – A , where x is the individual item,

Σ d = sum of deviations and n = number of items.

concepts.title: Arithmetic Mean
concepts.conceptBody:
Arithmetic Mean Based on Frequency

(i) Direct method : If the terms of the given series be ${{x}_{1}},\,{{x}_{2}},\,....,\,{{x}_{n}}$ and the corresponding frequencies be ${{f}_{1}},\,{{f}_{2}},\,....{{f}_{n}}$, then the arithmetic mean $\bar{x}$ is given by,

$\bar{x}=\frac{{{f}_{1}}{{x}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}}{{x}_{2}}+....+{{f}_{n}}{{x}_{n}}}{{{f}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}}+....+{{f}_{n}}}=\frac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{n}{{{f}_{i}}{{x}_{i}}}}{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{n}{{{f}_{i}}}}$.

(ii) Short cut method 

$(\bar{x})=A+\frac{\sum f(x-A)}{\sum f}$,

where A = assumed mean, f = frequency and x – A = deviation of each item from the assumed mean.

concepts.title: Geometric Mean
concepts.conceptBody:
Geometric mean

If ${{x}_{1}},\,{{x}_{2}},\,{{x}_{3}},\,......,\,{{x}_{n}}$ are n values of a variate x , none of them being zero, then geometric mean (G.M.) is given by

$G.M.={{\left( {{X}_{1}},{{X}_{2}}\cdot {{X}_{3}}\ldots \ldots ..{{X}_{n}} \right)}^{1/n}}$

⇒ $\log (G.M.)=\frac{1}{n}\left( \log {{x}_{1}}+\log {{x}_{2}}+\ldots ..+\log {{x}_{n}} \right)$.

In case of frequency distribution, G.M. of n values ${{x}_{1}},\,{{x}_{2}},\,.....{{x}_{n}}$ of a variate x occurring with frequency ${{f}_{1}},\,{{f}_{2}},\,.....,\,{{f}_{n}}$ is given by

$\text{ G}\text{.M}\text{. }={{\left( x_{1}^{1}x_{2}^{{{R}_{2}}}\ldots ..x_{n}^{{{l}_{n}}} \right)}^{1/N}}$, where $N={{f}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}}+.....+{{f}_{n}}$.

concepts.title: Harmonic Mean
concepts.conceptBody:
Harmonic mean

The harmonic mean of n items ${{x}_{1}},\,{{x}_{2}},\,......,\,{{x}_{n}}$ is defined as

$H.M.=\frac{n}{\frac{1}{{{x}_{1}}}+\frac{1}{{{x}_{2}}}+\ldots ..+\frac{1}{{{x}_{n}}}}$.

If the frequency distribution is ${{f}_{1}},\,{{f}_{2}},\,{{f}_{3}},\,......,\,{{f}_{n}}$ respectively, then

$\text{ H}\text{.M}\text{. }=\frac{{{f}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}}+{{f}_{3}}+\ldots ..+{{f}_{n}}}{\left( \frac{{{f}_{1}}}{{{x}_{1}}}+\frac{{{f}_{2}}}{{{x}_{2}}}+\ldots .+\frac{{{f}_{n}}}{{{x}_{n}}} \right)}$.
